issues.description: Finish consolidating the canonical Go task, intake, and workflow vocabulary so downstream runtime slices stop inventing parallel compatibility contracts.
issues.title: Unified domain model and intake contract migration

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Closed the remaining Go-owned risk/policy approval semantics gap by restoring Python parity for negative-budget manual-review scoring and wiring the canonical P0 audit-spec events into the Go mainline. The Go runtime now emits validated `execution.scheduler_decision`, `execution.manual_takeover`, `execution.flow_handoff`, and metadata-driven `execution.budget_override` events at decision time, while the Go workflow engine records `execution.approval_recorded` when approvals are supplied so the spec registry is backed by actual runtime/workflow traces rather than tests alone.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/observability/audit.go internal/risk/risk.go internal/risk/risk_test.go internal/worker/runtime.go internal/workflow/engine.go internal/worker/runtime_test.go internal/workflow/engine_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/risk ./internal/worker ./internal/workflow ./internal/observability` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA); `git log -1 --stat` (captured for commit `dd93645`).
Commit: `dd93645788ab87d555be7d8b89a1179dbd06a4c0`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

issues.description: Keep porting governance, execution-contract, audit, and policy semantics into Go so review and approval behavior no longer depends on Python-only code paths.
issues.title: Risk, policy, and approval semantics migration

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Completed the Go workflow execution slice by adding a workflow engine with journal/report closeout handling, exposing a `/v2/workflows/run` API endpoint that reuses the worker runtime, and wiring the daemon server to provide runtime-backed workflow execution.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/workflow ./internal/api` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass).
Commit: `68a25dc8d458010d65de75c07b7158f9feff24cc`.
PR URL: unavailable in this workspace because `origin` points to `/Users/jxrt/Desktop/symphony-main/BigClaw` rather than a GitHub remote.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Updated the Go orchestrator loop so each scheduler tick derives live quota inputs from the current queue instead of reusing the bootstrap snapshot. This now feeds queue depth, active leased work, and preemptible capacity into scheduler decisions, with a focused loop test covering live queued vs leased tasks.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/orchestrator/loop.go internal/orchestrator/loop_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/orchestrator ./internal/scheduler ./internal/worker ./internal/workflow` (pass).
Commit: `ffc682ea98227da27af1d2fc8256d2cd19059665`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Extended the Go workflow/scheduler runtime so takeover-blocked work is parked out of actionable queue capacity, approval-required work is blocked before executor dispatch, computed high-risk workflows also resolve to `needs-approval`, and approval blocks register in control state so the existing release path can unpark them later.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/queue ./internal/worker ./internal/orchestrator ./internal/api` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/scheduler ./internal/worker ./internal/workflow` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass).
Commit: `a6e2a9ea3fc6b8bf693d9d93c5936d595382f92f`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Updated the Go orchestrator loop to run an immediate startup pass and drain runnable queue work within a tick instead of processing only one task per poll interval. Added focused loop coverage for burst processing and for stop conditions when a task is deferred back onto the queue by scheduler policy.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/orchestrator/loop.go internal/orchestrator/loop_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/orchestrator ./internal/worker ./internal/scheduler ./internal/workflow` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass).
Commit: `1eaa5df1746fceccaff3e093202fcb63617807a0`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Updated the Go workflow engine so scheduler quota guardrails default from task policy when callers omit an explicit snapshot. Workflow runs now inherit policy concurrency, queue-depth, and budget ceilings, with coverage proving an over-budget workflow task is retried by the scheduler instead of bypassing controls.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/workflow/engine.go internal/workflow/engine_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/workflow ./internal/orchestrator ./internal/worker ./internal/scheduler` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass).
Commit: `18eff71bc63ed2f71c585d03d21532a86d8bff31`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Exposed the effective scheduler quota in Go workflow run artifacts so runtime closeout can explain the exact concurrency, queue-depth, and budget guardrails applied during execution. Workflow run results now return the resolved quota snapshot, execution journal entries capture it, and markdown reports render the same quota summary with focused assertions covering explicit and policy-derived quota paths.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/workflow/engine.go internal/workflow/engine_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/workflow ./internal/orchestrator ./internal/worker ./internal/scheduler` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA).
Commit: `c427ea147c787cb507c84fcacf6f481f950fbd2c`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Aligned the Go runtime with the execution contract by surfacing a stable `sandbox_profile` across scheduler and worker lifecycle events instead of exposing executor kind alone. Routed, approval-blocked, started, completed, cancelled, retried, and dead-letter payloads now carry a profile that reflects browser, container, distributed, or workspace-write execution paths, with worker coverage asserting the new payloads for browser, approval-gated, and dead-letter flows.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/worker/runtime.go internal/worker/runtime_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/worker ./internal/scheduler ./internal/workflow` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA).
Commit: `a27d739eda288582dec7555e0208ba7c8e75a57c`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Extended the Go run-detail API and markdown report to expose the runtime `sandbox_profile` carried by scheduler and worker events, so operator-visible execution artifacts now show whether work ran in browser, container, distributed, or workspace-write mode. Event-backed tool traces now return the sandbox profile field in JSON, and the downloadable run report renders the same profile inline for the execution timeline.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/api/v2.go internal/api/server_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/api ./internal/worker ./internal/workflow` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA).
Commit: `16c32263ecb14d44237378fc0579e9f066d6f23a`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Extended the Go workflow engine and `/v2/workflows/run` response so workflow callers now receive the resolved execution surface directly: the routed executor and sandbox profile are derived from emitted runtime events, included in the workflow result JSON, written into execution journal metadata, and rendered in the workflow markdown report. Focused workflow/API coverage now asserts the new response fields and report content for a successful local execution path.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/workflow/engine.go internal/workflow/engine_test.go internal/api/expansion_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/workflow ./internal/api ./internal/worker` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA).
Commit: `f609b5000e22d0078a60aac090ae7b93ecf02f82`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Lifted the resolved execution surface into the top-level Go run-detail summary so operator clients can read executor and sandbox profile directly without reconstructing them from tool traces. `/v2/runs/:id` now returns `executor` and `sandbox_profile` alongside the existing validation and risk summary, and the markdown run report header renders the same execution fields.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/api/v2.go internal/api/server_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/api ./internal/worker ./internal/workflow` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA).
Commit: `78787c8dbe3ce466297773a09041dc4efa13439d`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Fixed the Go workflow execution loop so explicit approval evidence now unblocks high-risk workflows instead of letting the scheduler/runtime re-park them before execution. The workflow engine stamps approval metadata onto queued tasks, the scheduler honors that metadata when evaluating risk gates, and both engine-level and `/v2/workflows/run` coverage now prove an approved high-risk workflow executes successfully and preserves approval refs in the response payload.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/scheduler/scheduler.go internal/scheduler/scheduler_test.go internal/workflow/engine.go internal/workflow/engine_test.go internal/api/expansion_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/scheduler ./internal/workflow ./internal/api ./internal/worker` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA).
Commit: `f49e99733ea6e8df873339e8788d9987cffef841`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Marked `BIG-GOM-303` done after closing the remaining Go workflow execution-loop gaps: live orchestrator quota snapshots, blocked-work parking, approval-gated execution, workflow run closeout artifacts, runtime/run-detail execution-surface reporting, and approved high-risk workflow resumption are now covered in Go-owned runtime, scheduler, orchestrator, workflow, and API paths. The remaining Python `workflow.py` features map to observability/report artifact surfaces already tracked under `BIG-GOM-304`, so active development no longer depends on the Python workflow engine for execution-loop behavior.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/queue ./internal/worker ./internal/orchestrator ./internal/api` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/scheduler ./internal/worker ./internal/workflow` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/api ./internal/worker ./internal/workflow` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA before tracker closeout commit).
Commit: `d68306ee31ea81e5badad7254bba0e30ab6f8300`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

issues.description: Close the Go execution loop with workflow-aware orchestration, journal state, and runtime closeout so active development no longer needs the Python workflow engine.
issues.title: Workflow orchestration and scheduler loop migration

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Added a Go-owned policy/prompt version center reporting surface and exposed it through `POST /v2/reports/policy-prompt-version-center/render`, so reporting clients can submit artifact revisions and receive both structured history data and markdown closeout output. Added unit coverage for diff/rollback semantics plus API coverage for rendered history and markdown, and updated module metadata for the `go-difflib` dependency.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/reporting/version_center.go internal/reporting/version_center_test.go internal/api/expansion.go internal/api/expansion_test.go internal/api/server.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go mod tidy` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/reporting ./internal/api ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA).

comments.body:
Added a Go-owned weekly operations artifact bundle writer and exposed it through `POST /v2/reports/weekly/bundle`, so reporting workflows can materialize the full weekly closeout pack in one call. The new bundle writes weekly report, operations dashboard, operations metric spec, regression center, queue control center, and optional policy/prompt version center markdown files to a requested root directory, with focused coverage for both the reporting writer and the API endpoint.

Weekly report payloads now include the bundle render URL plus the supported artifact set, with updated API coverage to assert the new discovery contract.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Marked `BIG-GOM-304` done after closing the remaining Go-owned runtime closeout and reviewer-evidence reporting gaps from the Python helper set. Weekly operations bundles, version center history, engineering/export surfaces, design-system report/bundle output, report studio artifacts, repo-sync audit, issue validation, pilot scorecard/portfolio, launch checklist, final delivery checklist, and issue closure decision reporting are now owned by Go packages and API endpoints, while the remaining Python report helpers align with `BIG-GOM-305` triage/control-center surfaces and `BIG-GOM-306` repo-collaboration surfaces rather than `BIG-GOM-304` acceptance.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/reporting ./internal/api` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA before tracker closeout commit).
Commit: `736b4ec97ba57c6319f8d3857df9fc843d43cc6d`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

issues.description: Move runtime evidence, reporting, and weekly operations closeout onto Go-owned observability outputs that line up with the new execution loop.
issues.title: Observability, reporting, and weekly operations surface migration

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Started `BIG-GOM-305` by adding a Go-owned triage center reporting surface on top of the existing triage JSON endpoint. `/v2/triage/center` now advertises export and bundle URLs, `GET /v2/triage/center/export` renders a markdown auto-triage report from the current center state, `POST /v2/triage/center/bundle` writes `triage-center.md`, and the triage package now owns the markdown renderer with focused coverage for queue and cluster summaries.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/triage/report.go internal/triage/report_test.go internal/reporting/bundle.go internal/reporting/bundle_test.go internal/api/v2.go internal/api/server.go internal/api/server_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/triage ./internal/reporting ./internal/api` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA); `git log -1 --stat --oneline` (captured for commit `7cf5212`).
Commit: `7cf52122a3e51930471cbd02bf8f9b624caa61b3`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

Extended the Go run-detail surface with an HTML detail export so operator clients now have parity with the Python task-run detail page instead of markdown-only reporting. `/v2/runs/:id` now advertises both markdown and HTML reports, and `GET /v2/runs/:id/detail` renders an HTML run detail page covering summary, linked reports, tool traces, artifacts, and event timeline with focused API coverage for the new report link and rendered page content.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/api/v2.go internal/api/server_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/api` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA); `git log -1 --stat --oneline` (captured for commit `aa8b735`).

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Added the remaining Go-owned control-center reporting discovery and bundle surface so `/v2/control-center` now advertises export and bundle URLs, `POST /v2/control-center/bundle` writes `control-center.md`, and the reporting package owns the new control-center bundle artifact with coverage alongside the API route tests for discovery and generated markdown content.

Added the remaining Go-owned saved-views and alert-digest reporting surface for `BIG-GOM-305`. The Go product package now owns the saved-view catalog, audit, readiness scoring, and markdown renderer from the Python contract; the reporting bundle helper now writes `saved-views.md`; and the API exposes `POST /v2/saved-views/render` plus `POST /v2/saved-views/bundle` with server coverage for preview discovery, audit output, and emitted bundle content.

Control-center report discovery/bundles, triage center export/bundle output, HTML run detail, operations dashboard coverage, and the saved-view/alert-digest render and bundle endpoints now give the Go mainline owners for the operator-facing status, triage, and control surfaces tracked by this slice.

comments.author: codex
comments.body: Claimed BIG-GOM-306 as the next parallel-safe slice after closing BIG-GOM-307. Next work will focus on repo collaboration and lineage surfaces that still need Go owners.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Started the first Go-owned repo collaboration slice by porting the shared repo plane, run-commit link binding, and registry contracts out of the Python implementation. `bigclaw-go/internal/repo` now owns `Space`, `Agent`, and `RunCommitLink` models plus accepted-commit binding and deterministic repo registry/channel resolution helpers, with focused coverage matching the Python `repo_plane.py`, `repo_links.py`, and `repo_registry.py` behavior.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/repo/plane.go internal/repo/links.go internal/repo/registry.go internal/repo/plane_test.go internal/repo/links_test.go internal/repo/registry_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/repo` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA for commit `cffd021`).

Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w internal/repo/commits.go internal/repo/gateway.go internal/repo/gateway_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/repo` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA for commit `5d156e3`).

Marked `BIG-GOM-306` done after porting the remaining repo collaboration and lineage Python surfaces listed in the cutover brief into Go-owned repo contracts. `bigclaw-go/internal/repo` now owns governance, repo plane models, run-commit links, deterministic registry/channel resolution, discussion board posts/replies, commit/lineage/diff models, and gateway normalization/audit helpers, so the repo collaboration surfaces required by the Go mainline no longer depend on `src/bigclaw/repo_*` implementations for active development.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Added Go-first local tracker mutation commands to `bigclawctl` so unattended workflows can update `local-issues.json` without manual JSON edits. `bigclawctl local-issues state` and `bigclawctl local-issues comment` now update state transitions, append timestamped comments, and preserve existing issue metadata through the same Go-owned refill/store layer.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w cmd/bigclawctl/main.go cmd/bigclawctl/main_test.go internal/refill/local_store.go internal/refill/local_store_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./cmd/bigclawctl ./internal/refill` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA for commit `49bfb69`).
Commit: `49bfb6907d7e5da51728603cd1768c1aff01c570`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Extended the Go-first local tracker toolchain so the operator wrapper no longer depends on Symphony for basic local issue management. `bigclawctl local-issues list` now exposes filtered tracker reads from `local-issues.json`, `scripts/ops/bigclaw-issue` routes `list`, `state`, and `comment` through the Go CLI, and the refill store now exposes safe issue snapshots for CLI output while preserving in-memory state isolation.
Validation: `cd bigclaw-go && gofmt -w cmd/bigclawctl/main.go cmd/bigclawctl/main_test.go internal/refill/local_store.go internal/refill/local_store_test.go` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./cmd/bigclawctl ./internal/refill` (pass); `bash scripts/ops/bigclaw-issue list | sed -n "1,5p"` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA for commit `7937279`).
Commit: `7937279280847904a3ee240e9f76cef8e239bd44`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Converted the remaining legacy ops compatibility scripts into Go-first shims instead of Python implementation entrypoints. `scripts/ops/bigclaw_github_sync.py`, `bigclaw_refill_queue.py`, `bigclaw_workspace_bootstrap.py`, `symphony_workspace_bootstrap.py`, and `symphony_workspace_validate.py` now forward into `scripts/ops/bigclawctl`, preserving the legacy script names while keeping GitHub sync, refill, and workspace bootstrap/validation on the Go toolchain path; executable mode was restored so direct shebang execution still works.
Validation: `python3 -m py_compile scripts/ops/bigclaw_github_sync.py scripts/ops/bigclaw_refill_queue.py scripts/ops/bigclaw_workspace_bootstrap.py scripts/ops/symphony_workspace_bootstrap.py scripts/ops/symphony_workspace_validate.py` (pass); `python3 scripts/ops/bigclaw_github_sync.py status --json` (pass); `python3 scripts/ops/bigclaw_refill_queue.py --local-issues local-issues.json` (pass); `./scripts/ops/bigclaw_github_sync.py status --json` (pass); `./scripts/ops/bigclaw_refill_queue.py --local-issues local-issues.json` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA after executable-bit fix).
Commit: `48f15f3818e1606c0fdcafc1e0f684bc58b0e433`, `d81c524e582c334c5714aed3596e46f875c0ed57`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Marked `BIG-GOM-307` done after finishing the remaining Go-first operator toolchain migration work. `bigclawctl` now owns local tracker mutation and listing, the `bigclaw-issue` wrapper routes local tracker verbs through the Go CLI, workflow hooks already run Go bootstrap/sync/refill flows, and the remaining legacy ops script names now forward into `scripts/ops/bigclawctl` while preserving direct executable compatibility. The Python code left behind is shim-only and aligns with the explicit `BIG-GOM-308` retirement follow-up rather than `BIG-GOM-307` acceptance.

comments.author: codex
comments.body: Claimed BIG-GOM-308 as the next slice after closing BIG-GOM-306. Next work will mark the remaining Python entrypoints as legacy and tighten the Go-only default path around bigclawd and bigclawctl.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Added the first Python-retirement marker slice by making the remaining Python CLI and static server entrypoints explicitly legacy-facing. `src/bigclaw/__main__.py` now advertises itself as migration-only, emits a legacy runtime notice for `serve` and `repo-sync-audit`, and points operators toward `bigclawd` and `bigclawctl`; `src/bigclaw/service.py` now carries the same legacy-service note when the Python server path is launched; and the root README now documents the preferred Go runtime/tooling path more explicitly.
Validation: `python3 -m py_compile src/bigclaw/__main__.py src/bigclaw/service.py` (pass); `PYTHONPATH=src python3 - <<"PY"
import bigclaw.__main__
import bigclaw.service
print("imports-ok")
PY` (pass); `python3 -m pytest --version` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass). Attempted `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m pytest tests/test_service.py -q` and direct single-test invocation, but the local pytest run aborted immediately with exit `-1` and no emitted output in this workspace, so I could not get runner-level evidence beyond compile/import checks.
Commit: `90a4aebc349e9fd2e2340b030efd6c708b37be8e`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Marked the remaining Python packaging/bootstrap developer path as legacy-facing to reinforce the Go-only default path. `setup.py` and `pyproject.toml` now describe the Python package as a legacy migration surface, `scripts/dev_bootstrap.sh` prints an explicit legacy warning before provisioning the Python environment, `scripts/dev_smoke.py` now emits the same notice before running the scheduler smoke check, and the root README now makes that helper path clearly migration-only.
Validation: `python3 -m py_compile src/bigclaw/__main__.py src/bigclaw/service.py scripts/dev_smoke.py` (pass); `PYTHONPATH=src python3 scripts/dev_smoke.py` (pass); `python3 setup.py --name` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass).
Commit: `342369956a81e66c9c0809acf63a154796012040`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Marked `BIG-GOM-308` done after removing Python from the default developer and runtime path while leaving explicit legacy markers where compatibility is still intentional. `workflow.md`, `scripts/ops/bigclawctl`, the Git hooks, and the repo docs already point operators at Go-first bootstrap/sync/refill/service entrypoints; `src/bigclaw/__main__.py`, `src/bigclaw/service.py`, `scripts/dev_bootstrap.sh`, `scripts/dev_smoke.py`, and package metadata now all identify the Python path as migration-only instead of a default runtime. The remaining Python entrypoints are retained as legacy or compatibility shims rather than active mainline surfaces.
Validation: `python3 -m py_compile src/bigclaw/__main__.py src/bigclaw/service.py scripts/dev_smoke.py` (pass); `PYTHONPATH=src python3 - <<"PY"
import bigclaw.__main__
import bigclaw.service
print("imports-ok")
PY` (pass); `PYTHONPATH=src python3 scripts/dev_smoke.py` (pass); `python3 setup.py --name` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` (pass). Attempted focused Python pytest validation for `tests/test_service.py`, but the local pytest invocation exited `-1` immediately with no emitted output in this workspace, so compile/import/smoke checks are the available Python-side evidence here.
Commit: `90a4aebc349e9fd2e2340b030efd6c708b37be8e`, `342369956a81e66c9c0809acf63a154796012040`.

comments.author: codex

First pass will inventory and mark those helper entrypoints as legacy/migration-only so the Go mainline does not silently depend on Python validation surfaces that are outside the runtime/toolchain cutover already closed by BIG-GOM-307/BIG-GOM-308.

comments.body:
Started `BIG-GOM-309` by creating a tracked follow-up slice for the remaining Python-backed helper surfaces still living under `bigclaw-go/scripts`, updating the refill metadata to include the new issue, and marking the first migration-shadow and benchmark entrypoints as explicit legacy validation tooling instead of silent Go-mainline dependencies. `scripts/migration/shadow_compare.py`, `scripts/migration/shadow_matrix.py`, `scripts/benchmark/run_matrix.py`, and `scripts/benchmark/soak_local.py` now emit migration-only notices on invocation, while `bigclaw-go/docs/migration-shadow.md`, `bigclaw-go/docs/e2e-validation.md`, and `bigclaw-go/docs/benchmark-plan.md` now call out that these Python helpers are evidence tooling under active audit rather than mainline runtime/operator entrypoints.

comments.author: codex
comments.body: Claimed BIG-GOM-310 to reconcile the repo-native planning metadata after BIG-GOM-309 closed. The local tracker is complete through BIG-GOM-309, but `docs/parallel-refill-queue.json`, `docs/parallel-refill-queue.md`, and the issue-pack current-status summary still advertise older active slices, so this follow-up will make the refill/project-closeout docs match the actual local tracker state before the branch is handed off.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Reconciled the repo-native planning metadata with the local tracker after the BIG-GOM-301 through BIG-GOM-309 tranche completed. `docs/parallel-refill-queue.json` now marks completed slices accurately, carries `BIG-GOM-310` as the active closeout metadata follow-up, and extends the canonical issue order; `docs/parallel-refill-queue.md` now reflects the completed tranche vs current active slice; and `docs/go-mainline-cutover-issue-pack.md` now has an explicit current local tracker status section that points readers at the live local tracker and refill queue for active state instead of the historical draft recommendations below.

Validation: `python3 -m json.tool docs/parallel-refill-queue.json` (pass); `python3 -m json.tool local-issues.json` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go run ./cmd/bigclawctl local-issues list --store ../local-issues.json --json | rg -n "BIG-GOM-310|"state": "In Progress"|"state": "Done""` (pass); `rg -n "BIG-GOM-310|BIG-GOM-309|Current batch|Current local tracker status|completed|active" docs/parallel-refill-queue.json docs/parallel-refill-queue.md docs/go-mainline-cutover-issue-pack.md` (pass); `git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-303` (same SHA for commit `e2f2d6fc7a991909d120c93e42ce99cbf87e1b14`).

Commit: `e2f2d6fc7a991909d120c93e42ce99cbf87e1b14`.

comments.author: codex
comments.body: Claimed BIG-GOM-315 to eliminate the remaining self-reopening closeout drift. The top-level current-state docs still hardcode the latest completed ticket identifier, which guarantees another mismatch every time a reconciliation slice closes, so this follow-up will switch those summaries to stable tracker-based wording instead of repeating exact tranche boundary numbers.

comments.author: codex
comments.body:
Removed the self-reopening closeout drift from the top-level current-state docs. `README.md` and `docs/go-mainline-cutover-issue-pack.md` no longer hardcode the latest completed ticket identifier; they now point readers at `local-issues.json` and `docs/parallel-refill-queue.json` as the live source of truth while keeping the terminal-closeout status explicit. That means future closeout reconciliation slices will not invalidate the landing summaries just by incrementing the highest completed ticket number.
Validation: `python3 -m json.tool local-issues.json` (pass); `python3 -m json.tool docs/parallel-refill-queue.json` (pass); `cd bigclaw-go && go run ./cmd/bigclawctl local-issues list --store ../local-issues.json --json | rg -n 'BIG-GOM-315|"state": "In Progress"'` (pass before closeout); `rg -n 'BIG-GOM-313|BIG-GOM-314|BIG-GOM-315|complete through|runs through|live source of truth|repo-local migration tranche recorded in `local-issues.json` is complete|terminal closeout state' README.md docs/go-mainline-cutover-issue-pack.md docs/parallel-refill-queue.md docs/parallel-refill-queue.json` (pass after wording change).
Commit: `f5d7bbd85ae0b10bdd2ea2adbe9154f2202d542d`, `175082198fa8a03940840b607d950a066b9ee047`.
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/139.
